title: City Water Distribution Simulator
description: Manage a virtual city's water supply by placing, filling, and routing water bottles across district nodes. The city has a fixed total water budget governed by a conservation law — filling one district drains another. Users must balance distribution, undo mistakes, and satisfy minimum thresholds for all districts simultaneously.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements a city water distribution simulation. Load VirtualCity.glb centered on the scene as the base terrain. Load WaterBottle.glb and clone it 5 times, placing each bottle at a labeled district node (A–E) hovering above distinct areas of the city model. Use OrbitControls for camera navigation. Set initial camera at position (0, 12, 18) looking at origin. Add hemisphere light (sky 0x87CEEB, ground 0x444444, intensity 1.0) and a directional light casting soft shadows.

Core mechanics: A global water budget of 100 units is conserved — the sum of all 5 bottles must always equal 100. Each bottle displays its current water level (0–50) via a floating HTML label and a blue transparent cylinder inside the bottle that scales vertically to represent fill level. Clicking a bottle selects it (outline glow via emissive). A slider panel appears allowing transfer of water FROM the selected bottle TO any other bottle in increments of 5 units. Transferring water animates the source bottle's fill shrinking and the target's fill growing over 0.5 seconds. Each district has a random minimum threshold (10–25) shown in red text; the bottle label turns green when its level meets or exceeds the threshold.

Interaction steps: (1) orbit to survey city, (2) click bottle A, (3) transfer 10 units to bottle C, (4) click bottle B, (5) transfer 15 units to bottle D, (6) undo last transfer via an Undo button (restores previous state from a history stack), (7) redo via Redo button, (8) click bottle E, (9) transfer 5 units to bottle A, (10) click a Reset button to redistribute evenly (20 each), (11-12) make final adjustments so all districts meet thresholds. Display a top banner showing total water (always 100) and a success indicator that lights up when all thresholds are met. Maintain an undo/redo history stack of at least 10 states. All UI controls should be HTML overlay elements styled with semi-transparent dark panels.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  bottleAWater: number,         // current water units in district A bottle (0-50)
  bottleBWater: number,         // current water units in district B bottle (0-50)
  bottleCWater: number,         // current water units in district C bottle (0-50)
  bottleDWater: number,         // current water units in district D bottle (0-50)
  bottleEWater: number,         // current water units in district E bottle (0-50)
  selectedBottleId: string,         // ID of currently selected bottle (A-E) or empty
  totalWaterBudget: number,         // global water conservation constant (always 100)
  transferInProgress: boolean,         // true when water transfer animation is active
  districtAThreshold: number,         // minimum water requirement for district A (10-25)
  districtBThreshold: number,         // minimum water requirement for district B (10-25)
  districtCThreshold: number,         // minimum water requirement for district C (10-25)
  districtDThreshold: number,         // minimum water requirement for district D (10-25)
  districtEThreshold: number,         // minimum water requirement for district E (10-25)
}
